note.body: There's more in the `treeToSearialized`, `stringToTree` and `treeToString`, function. Also don't know if you want to put them all at the top of the file? (again, I'm good with it either way). There's a few types just sitting above the function definition.
Thanks again. All comments addressed and fixed.
I'm not okay with that :) I pulled them out.
I turned on inline test coverage gutter colors and this was leftover when getting that to work. Apologies. I've removed it.
Good call. Don't know what I was thinking.
Good eye. They should all be `0.8rem`. I've changed the tab font size to match the Channels dropdown.

push_data.commit_title: Snapshot -> Layout; url decode of layout send directly do dockview ...

note.body: I've didn't even know you could define types in the middle of a function. I can learn to get used to it. Wanted to double check you are okay with that.
what happened that this was needed?

I can't get my window to show like your screenshot. I see the dropdown overflow but I still can't get to the "+" button or the settings button

![image.png](/uploads/f3f289cd572f12668b9469a1fe2a5b4f/image.png){width=362 height=157}
I am still seeing this issue? Happy to hop on a screen share.
I'm good with it. I like the smaller share-links for sure. I didn't dig heavily into the builder functions.
Feels alright to me!

The font size is still looking slightly larger in the right tab controls for me. Was that intentional or are they supposed to be exactly the same? Font size on the panel picker dropdown is reading `12px` and font size for the comm channel dropdown is reading `12.8px` 

Font size on the channel picker for the video component is 14px (incase you intended that one to match as well)
I don't think you need to pass in `dockViewApi` as a param here, correct? I think `generateShareURL` can just access the DocviewApi on it's own inside the function?
Yes! I LOVE the new names for the components. They always felt awkward to me.
Awesome thanks! I love that a lot better.

Ah okay cool that makes sense. They aren't called frames anymore, right?

```typescriptreact
  /** Select a layout preset and trim Redux framecount to match the new panel count.
   * This stops pane states from being orphaned when the number of panes decreases */
```

So this should read "... and trim the Redux panel count... " or is it "pane count"? Not sure which.
Love it!

Your call on that one. I've gone back and forth on that exact thought several times in the new key/map objects I've added to the AEGIS automerge branch. In my case I did create a new typing for that one line, but I'm totally okay with how you've got it now.

Also AEGIS, I confirmed that we do use the singular/plural pattern described for object maps. Take a look - `EquipmentItems`, `GeographicUnits` and `CircleDefinitions` are just a few. There are more: https://eegitlab.fit.nasa.gov/emss/aegis/-/blob/int/src/typings/mission.d.ts?ref_type=heads#L111

(you can close this thread after you've read this)

Works for me.

Btw you don't have to call it `[key: string]` and can totally just call it what it is... `[paneInstanceId: string]` in all the places you use it. It might add more clarity.

(you can close this thread after you've read this)

I understand why you would want to add this, and I agree they should not be in the repo. I'm trying to highlight that these artifacts are specific to an individual's development process and don't apply to the whole project/team. It would be better if there was another solution to manage your dev process. I went ahead and did some searching on my suggestion above and discovered this local `exlude` file. It might be better to put the extensions here since they only apply for you, and keep the repo `.gitignore` more generic.

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/22906851/when-would-you-use-git-info-exclude-instead-of-gitignore-to-exclude-files

If/when we adopt AI agents more formally on the repo as an expected way to dev code, we could rediscuss if it's appropriate to add to the .gitignore file.

Thanks so much for making this all better.

I also got rid of the snapshot in redux. It's now in `dockview-api-ref.ts` with a comment explaining why it was necessary. Basically it was a way to set a pending dockview layout from a shareURL before dockview was ready to receive it upon initial load.

I put dv at the end of the share urls and added safeguards against dv params that don't decode properly.

Actually, since writing the above I took a shot at expanding the DSL system to encode / decode any layout so we can use it for share links. This means the dv param is in plain text and isn't too long. This is in its own commit if you want to take a look at just this. Open to discussion about it. I like it.

Share links now look like `http://localhost:3000/view/?date=2026-02-28&gmt=15:08:02&s=0&v=3.0&f1=01001&f2=01011&f3=0300&f4=0701&f5=051&f6=04&f7=08&dv=v(h(1:553,2:553,3:555):465,h(5:415,6:415,7:351,4:478):366)`
Not terrible.
Yep. Another miss. Thanks!
Done!

Pane picker now closes when you pick a pane or if you click away. other component controls now close when you click away. Clicking away in all cases no longer triggers whatever is under the click (like video going to full screen).

I left the pane controls staying up as you interact with it since they are not dropdowns. Making them disappear on click feels wrong to me. Give it a shot.
16 files changed to `groupDimensions`. I kinda wanted "panelDimensions" but the size is literally sourced directly from the dockview group so `groupDimensions` it is.
Thanks again. Done!
Hawkeye. Done!

It was hard to say goodbye to my old friend/nemesis, the vertical dropdown on the coda video component. It's gone! You're so right that we don't need this anymore. It's so much better with the modal full channel selector now.

I also took a step back and looked at what we call these video components. Instead of "Video Channels" and "Video Other" they are now "Video Downlink" and "Video Non-Downlink". Much more clear and no longer a mystery as to why anyone would ever pick "other".
Thanks, I missed this one in the rename. Done!

Ok I took a shot at this. Turns out dockview has an automatic dropdown overflow for this case. It looks like this
![image](/uploads/40af50a7c97344dcfbb608e4a4676da7/image.png){width=559 height=88}
Rebuilt these so they match. Looks so nice.
This is fixed with that new dockview dropdowny thing that shows hidden panel tabs. mentioned above.
done

Fixed the spacing in the video controls and also spruced up comm controls, fixing the fonts and spacing as we discussed. Looks snazzy.
![image](/uploads/5dc343a3f5058e65da6eec3dd1ba4da2/image.png){width=430 height=373}

Good call. You're so much better at typescript now :smile: 

Made `type Panes = Record<PaneType, Pane>;` in framework.d.ts
Ok made this `LayoutLetter`

Added some clarification. Essentially when you reduce the number of visible panes when you select a different layout (or delete a panel / group) we need to kill the pane states that no longer apply so that when we add a new blank panel somewhere it doesn't inherit what used to be in that `paneInstanceId` <- (btw I just had to go look up what "frameId" is called now. My brain is so inflexible).
done!
Fixed! it was the header and index body

The plural means array for us (at least it does for me). This isn't an array like those AEGIS ones. So instead of plural, I just change every instance of `PaneInstaceState` to `{[key: string]: PaneState;}` Do you agree that that's fine? I feel like there's no reason to come up with a word that just means that one line.
Changed it to something obvious rather than attempt to navigate pane/paneInstance/panel/group world. I hope you like it.

Unfortunately AI agents receive instructions by putting these files in specific places in a project. I didn't think we are ready to commit these to our repo, so I'm ignoring them. I don't want to have to manually move them in and out of the repo every time I work on CODA, so I'd like to keep this.
done!
I'll remove it. I wanted my IDE to stop lagging. Should have just done it in my own prefs.

note.body: Add a safety net that if the dockview snapshot is truncated in the URL, it will just load the default view for that date.
I don't think this is `frameState` anymore, correct? It should be paneInstanceState?
I'd remove the `frame-` prefix from here on the id value, or rename it to "paneInstance"

A leftover "frame" in the prop `frameDimensions`. I think this is the dimension of the panel (or group? not sure what the correct term is here). I see that it is called "frameDimensions" all throughout the control components too.
I'd remove the `frame-` prefix from here on the id value, or rename it to "paneInstance"
"Frame" slipped through for `addFrame` and `removeFrame` - rename to pane? Or paneInstance I think?
Another spot here going on an earlier comment that if we can strongly type these pane type for the keys then they don't have to be a `string`

The font sizing difference between the panel setting dropdowns and the pane picker dropdown is kind of striking. For example see the talkybot panel. You have "Comms" in a dropdown with a tiny font, and then "Channels" dropdown in a larger font. To be silly, it feels like channels is yelling at me, lol. The styling on all the dropdowns should be the same imo. Might need to brainstorm something with Charney?

![image.png](/uploads/6deecef512df6c628d316619a765c1ae/image.png){width=900 height=457}

I think here I expected the video channel dropdown in the right panel to show up with a settings icon (like the left panel). Instead it turned into a vertical drop down. I like your dialog settings icon popup way better. Think we can get rid of this vertical drop down?

![image.png](/uploads/1779f1ca259b46a16785664b5e5e800b/image.png){width=567 height=235}

In certain configurations, the settings icon is cut off and there is no horizontal scroll bar showing here.

![image.png](/uploads/d33c8bd34db98b6dcbbaae9a772b0d31/image.png){width=422 height=264}

When I have enough tabs open in a panel to generate a horizontal scroll bar, it doesn't scroll far enough over for me to see the + button and the settings icon to change the channels

![image.png](/uploads/5861495ec38acc883876b1f5961b6cc6/image.png){width=351 height=206}
if the `layout` param is just the `allLayoutLetters`, is there any way to make `allLayoutLetters` some kind of type so we can pass it in here instead of a generic `string`?
I'm not sure I understand what this comment is saying by "trim Redux frames". Can you expand it more or rephrase it? _`/** Select a layout preset and trim Redux frames to match its panel count. */`_
update comment to "pane"
update comment to "list of panes"

Opening this on my regular NASA laptop screen I get a horizontal and vertical scrollbar. I confirmed my browser zoom is at 100%

![image.png](/uploads/dca56906a78d80ab1d242f89fadf3346/image.png){width=900 height=535}

Typing suggestion - is there a way to strongly type these Panes? Instead of a key being just a `string` here: https://eegitlab.fit.nasa.gov/emss/coda/-/blob/dockview/src/typings/framework.d.ts#L27 if it can be strongly typed you can also use that same typing for the `paneType: string` here https://eegitlab.fit.nasa.gov/emss/coda/-/blob/dockview/src/typings/framework.d.ts#L72, correct?

Minor comment - instead of `PaneInstanceState` this could be `PaneStates` to match the singular/plural patterns we use with key/value objects in AEGIS. At the very least it should probably be plural since it holds multiple states
is this comment supposed to say "pane instances to pane types?"

I'm assuming this was added because your local dev process was creating these? I wouldn't add them here to the project if they are artifacts due to how a individual develops code. Is there some kind of .vscode user configuration ignore file? I would only put here things that the project generates.
update this comment since you removed .env.local below
Did you mean to just disable this locally?
